name: Goias (Brazil)
description: Eight equal horizontal stripes alternating yellow and green, with a blue canton in the upper hoist bearing five white five-pointed stars in the pattern of the Southern Cross.
history: Designed by the poet Joaquim Bonifacio Gomes de Siqueira and adopted as the flag of the State of Goias by Decree No. 600 of 30 July 1919.

features.symbolism: Eight equal horizontal stripes alternating yellow and green; the colours are those of the Brazilian national flag
Blue canton bearing the five stars of the Southern Cross
the constellation of the Southern Cross seen over Goias
